Delhi's forgotten 1,100-year-old dam still tells the story of the TomarsChasing the early history of Delhi, the India Today team reached its southernmost edge. Hidden in the Aravallis stands a dam built around 1,100 years ago, a structure that tells the forgotten story of early Delhi and the Tomars, among the region's earliest known rulers. The village of Anangpur was one of the earliest settlements in the area and is believed to have later served as a Tomar capital. This small region carries many layers of history, and what we found there was eye-opening. Join us this week as we uncover this historical treasure.

BJP's Nishikant Dubey's remark against Biju Patnaik sparks resignation, outrageNishikant Dubey, the head of the Parliamentary standing committee on Communications and IT, allegedly called Biju Patnaik a CIA agent, which triggered a major controversy.

Live: Iran calls US proposal 'unrealistic', says no direct talks heldUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: As the Iran-US-Israel conflict intensifies, multiple flashpoints are emerging across the region, signalling a dangerous escalation. Hundreds of US Special Operations forces have arrived in the Middle East, pushing total American troop levels beyond 50,000 as President Donald Trump weighs options ranging from securing the Strait of Hormuz to targeting Iran's nuclear assets. Trump has also floated the idea of seizing Iran's oil infrastructure and enriched uranium, even as backchannel diplomacy continues via regional intermediaries. Meanwhile, Iran's Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei remains out of public view, fuelling speculation over his health and grip on power. The conflict has begun spilling beyond traditional battle zones, with an Indian worker killed in an Iranian strike in Kuwait, underscoring risks to civilians and critical infrastructure. Experts warn the situation is increasingly volatile, with potential for wider regional fallout. Stay with IndiaToday.in for the latest and live updates.

New Covid-19 variant Cicada confirmed: How did it form?For India, experts advise vigilance but not alarm. The country has built considerable immunity through widespread vaccination and prior waves of infection.

Iran war is pushing US to its economic limits. How long can it fight on?The Iran war has entered its fifth week with no end in sight. While the global energy crisis is deepening as the war progresses, the US has largely been spared, except for higher prices at fuel stations. The question is, how long America can absorb these costs before ordinary citizens start to feel the pinch?

Mojtaba Khamenei resurfaces with message to Iraq after Trump claimIran's Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei has thanked Iraq's people and religious leadership for supporting Tehran, according to state media, in his latest message. Live: Vijay calls DMK, CM Stalin 'evil forces' over lawlessness, drug abuseAssembly Election News 2026 Live Updates: Campaigning has intensified across Assam, Bengal, Kerala, Tamil Nadu and Puducherry with just weeks left for polling. On Monday, Prime Minister Narendra Modi will interact with the BJP cadres in Assam and Puducherry as part of the 'Mera Booth, Sabse Mazboot Samvaad' initiative, aiming to give messages directly to them ahead of assembly elections. This came after the PM said that in both states, the ruling National Democratic Alliance would secure a victory. Meanwhile, in Tamil Nadu, Chief Minister MK Stalin and TVK leader and actor-politician Vijay have filed their nominations today. Vijay will also hold a rally in Tamil Nadu's Perambur, from where he is about to contest. Catch all the live updates on indiatoday.in

Tottenham Hotspur sack interim manager Igor Tudor amid relegation threatTottenham Hotspur decided to sack interim manager Igor Tudor after a poor run of results that sees the club sit just one point above the relegation zone in the Premier League. Tudor survived for just six weeks at the helm.
